issuesFound.title: Missing language attribute on html element
issuesFound.explanation: The <html> element lacks a lang attribute, preventing screen readers from identifying the page's primary language and applying correct pronunciation rules. This affects all users relying on assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2: <html lang="en">

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button with class "nav-toggle" contains only a span with aria-hidden="true", resulting in no accessible name for screen reader users. They cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 33: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Toggle navigation">

issuesFound.title: Search submit button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button inside the search form contains only an image with aria-hidden="true", providing no accessible name. Screen reader users cannot identify it as a search action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 66: <button type="submit" aria-label="Search">

issuesFound.title: Main navigation landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple <nav> elements exist on the page, but this instance lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 63: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Account navigation landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple <nav> elements exist on the page, but this instance lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 83: <nav aria-label="Account actions">

issuesFound.title: Hero image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element for the hero dashboard lacks an alt attribute, making it invisible to screen readers. Users miss contextual information about the product interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100:

issuesFound.title: Nova Health logo image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element for the Nova Health logo lacks an alt attribute. Screen reader users cannot identify the brand represented in the social proof section.
issuesFound.fix: Line 113:

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference on features section
issuesFound.explanation: The section uses aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document. Screen readers cannot resolve the label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 132: <section aria-label="Features overview" class="features-section">

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text "Learn more"
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Learn more" is generic and does not describe the destination without surrounding context. Screen reader users navigating by links may not know where it leads.
issuesFound.fix: Line 146: Learn more about the scanner

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip from h2 to h5
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 (Line 166) directly to h5, skipping h3 and h4. This disrupts the logical outline for users navigating by headings.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 193:

Connect your project

issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Click here" is generic and does not describe the destination. Screen reader users navigating by links may not know where it leads.

issuesFound.title: Table header "Feature" missing scope attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element lacks a scope attribute in a table with multiple headers per row. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine if it applies to a column or row.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element lacks a scope attribute in a table with multiple headers per row. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine if it applies to a column or row.

issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element lacks a scope attribute in a table with multiple headers per row. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine if it applies to a column or row.

issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element lacks a scope attribute in a table with multiple headers per row. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine if it applies to a column or row.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users relying on focus indicators will not see where focus is located.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Button elements are critical interactive controls, and removing the focus ring blocks keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus state removes the outline without a visible alternative. Users navigating by keyboard cannot identify the logo link as focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus without replacement. This button is essential for mobile keyboard users to access the menu.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus within the dropdown menu.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the button as focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify which link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the link within the card.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the active filter control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the billing period control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the scrollable region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify which question is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the link as focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the link as focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the link as focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the checkbox as focused.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This exposes internal labels or skip links to sighted users inappropriately.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.9:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p text uses #aaaaaa on a white background, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Users with low vision may not read the description.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 1.8:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p text uses #94a3b8 on a #555555 background, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum. Users with low vision may not read the footer text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #64748b / background #555555 ratio 1.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p text uses #64748b on a #555555 background, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum. Users with low vision may not read the copyright text.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets a min-width and min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the icon button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has a height of 28px with no padding, which is below the 44px recommended size and relies on 24px minimum. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating it.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has a width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the checkbox.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition that is not disabled in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unwanted movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses a transform transition that is not disabled in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unwanted movement.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with cognitive or vision disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration, relying on colour alone to distinguish links. Users with colour blindness may not identify links.

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle visibility classes and emit events but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the navigation drawer opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2650: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 2675: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialization does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function locates the trigger element but does not initialize its aria-expanded attribute to 'false'. Assistive technologies may announce an incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2725: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the dropdown state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2835: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 2848: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu registration does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup but fails to set the initial aria-expanded='false' on the trigger. The trigger's state is undefined on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2925: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function updates the panel visibility and classes but does not set aria-expanded='true' on the item.trigger. The close() function correctly sets it to 'false', creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4335: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input does not toggle aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module's _open() and _close() functions manage the suggestion list visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the _inputEl (combobox). Screen readers will not know when the list is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3290: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 3300: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function configures the input as a combobox with aria-controls but does not set the initial aria-expanded='false'. The combobox state is incomplete on initialization.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3175: setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function toggles the visual checked state on _toggleTrack but does not update the aria-checked attribute on the _toggleEl (which has role='switch'). The switch state is not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3743: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, isYear);

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() function toggles the active class on filter buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Screen reader users cannot identify the active filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3535: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function adds error descriptions but fails to set aria-invalid='true' on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1945: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes error descriptions but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute. Fields may remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1955: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the product count badge but does not announce the change to a live region. Users are not informed that the filter results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3455: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates prices dynamically but does not announce the billing period change to a live region. Users are not informed of the price update context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3705: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick() function scrolls the page and moves focus but does not announce the action to a live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3135: liveRegion.announce('Scrolled back to top of page');

fpTitles: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Since there are multiple <nav> landmarks on the page (main, mobile, footer), this landmark must be distinguished for screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: Loading spinner icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The <span> element rendering the visual spinner inside the Button component does not have aria-hidden="true". While the text "Loading..." is visually hidden, the spinner itself is exposed to assistive technology as empty content, creating noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 426: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Star rating widget missing role="img"
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component uses a <span> with an aria-label to convey the rating value, but it lacks role="img". Without this role, screen readers may not treat the composite widget as a single image-like object, potentially announcing internal structure unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: <span role="img" aria-label={`${labelPrefix}: ${value} out of ${max} stars`}>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav id="main-nav"> element lacks an aria-label attribute. As one of several navigation landmarks on the page, it requires a unique label (e.g., "Main") to allow users to distinguish it from mobile or footer navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 567: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button controlling the mobile navigation sub-sections tracks state via openSectionId but does not receive aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot perceive the open/closed state of these accordion-like regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation body landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element inside the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label attribute. This landmark should be distinguished from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596: <nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component used for the theme toggle contains only an icon (emoji) and does not receive the required aria-label prop. The button has no accessible name, making its purpose unknown to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 683: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for the hero area lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Major page sections should be labelled to facilitate landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 706: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Hero video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button controlling the background video play/pause state tracks isPlaying but uses only a dynamic aria-label. It should also use aria-pressed to explicitly convey the toggle state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.title: Stats bar section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for the statistics bar lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Although it contains a visually hidden heading, the section element itself should reference it for proper landmark labeling.
issuesFound.fix: Line 803: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> element inside PlanCard lacks an aria-labelledby attribute referencing the plan name heading (id={headingId}). This prevents the card from having a distinct accessible name in the grid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1225: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Integrations bar duplicate list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second <ul> in IntegrationsBar is a visual clone for seamless looping but is not hidden from assistive technology. This causes screen readers to announce the same list of integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 853: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element with role="dialog" lacks aria-modal="true". The attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div instead. This prevents screen readers from understanding that interaction is limited to the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1541: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

fpTitles: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
